name: shadcn/ui principles & aesthetic
description: Minimalism, consistent defaults, strategic depth, accessibility-first, composition/modularity, responsive patterns, subtle motion, business-grade clarity.

metadata.intent: Use shadcn/ui’s design philosophy to keep interfaces clean, accessible, and composable

must_not: Decorative complexity that hides core tasks
conventions: Defaults should look professional out-of-the-box; customization stays in your repo.

steps.description: Minimalism: remove non-essential ornamentation; use whitespace, clean lines, and typography to focus attention on content and tasks.
Strategic depth: use shadows sparingly to establish hierarchy and affordance; interactive elevation changes should feel subtle and intentional.
Beautiful defaults & consistency: components should fit together without additional styling; prefer a small set of variants and a consistent token layer.
Accessibility-first: don’t remove focus styles; preserve Radix semantics; ensure keyboard navigation and screen reader labels remain correct.
Customization & flexibility: treat shadcn components as source code you own—copy, adapt, and extend within your repo; avoid creating wrapper abstraction layers too early.
Composition & modularity: compose predictable primitives into blocks; import only what you need; keep bundle lightweight by avoiding global mega-components.
Adaptive design: build mobile-first and ensure components behave well across breakpoints; test interactive targets and layout density on small screens.
Fluid animations: micro-interactions should clarify state changes (open/close, success/error) and never block task completion; honor reduced-motion preferences.
Business-oriented clarity: default to professionalism for internal tools and serious apps—clear hierarchy, readable density, consistent data presentation.
